title: Introduction
text: This website contains open-access tools for hydrological resource estimations. The applications, packages, and functions developed are intended to quickly and easily apply different statistical analyses and estimations using standard methodologies in water resource analysis. These tools aim to support decision-making related to hydrological risks, advanced statistical analysis of water resources, availability estimations, and more, through platforms and functions that allow for data management, proper visualization, and the download of all results.

title: About Me
text:
This website was designed by Alonso Arriagada to support hydrological estimations and education.
Any suggestion or question is welcome, please contact me by email: alonso.arriagada@usach.cl

title: Packages
text:
Estimation of time of concentration and lag times for watersheds based on their morphometric characteristics. It includes various methods for calculation and offers plotting functionalities for comparative analysis.
https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/Time.R/index.html

title: Packages
text:
Estimation of time of concentration and lag times for watersheds based on their morphometric characteristics. It includes various methods for calculation and offers plotting functionalities for comparative analysis.
https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/Time.R/index.html

title: Packages
text:
DeductiveR
Apply the Deductive Rational Method to a monthly series of hydrological data to fill in missing data.
https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/DeductiveR/index.html

title: Packages
text:
hydroDCindex
Compute duration curves of daily flow series, both real and modeled, to be compared through indexes of flow duration curves. The package functions include comparative plots and goodness of fit tests.
https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/hydroDCindex/index.html

title: Packages
text:
MCTrend
Application of a test to rule out that trends detected in hydrological time series are explained exclusively by the randomness of the climate.
https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/MCTrend/index.html
